title: Procurement Health Report Card
subtitle: How healthy is Mexico's public procurement?
nationalGradeExplain: Based on {{count}} internationally recognized indicators from the OECD, EU, and IMF.
sectionIndicatorsSubtitle: Each indicator is compared against international benchmarks used by the OECD, European Union, and IMF to evaluate procurement transparency worldwide.
sectionSectorsSubtitle: How does each government sector perform? Click any card for details.
sectionTrend: How Has It Changed Over Time?
sectionTrendSubtitle: National procurement health indicators from 2010 to 2025.
sectionAgreement: Do Simple Indicators Agree with AI?
sectionAgreementSubtitle: When both simple rules and machine learning flag the same contracts, confidence increases.
agreementStat: {{pct}}% of AI high-risk contracts also trigger at least one simple red flag
agreementExplain: Out of {{total}} contracts flagged as high-risk by the ML model, {{flagged}} also show at least one PHI red flag (direct award or single bid). This convergence validates both approaches.
competitionRateDesc: Percentage of contracts awarded through competitive procedures (not direct award). Higher is better.
singleBidRateDesc: Percentage of competitive procedures where only one vendor submitted a bid. Lower is better.
avgBiddersDesc: Average number of vendors competing per procedure. More competition means better prices and less corruption risk.
hhiDesc: Herfindahl-Hirschman Index measuring vendor concentration. Lower means more distributed spending.
shortAdRateDesc: Percentage of competitive procedures advertised for less than 15 days, limiting vendor participation.
amendmentRateDesc: Percentage of contracts modified after award (convenio modificatorio). High rates suggest poor planning or scope creep.
methodologyText: This report card uses the Procurement Health Index (PHI), a rule-based assessment requiring no machine learning. Every indicator is internationally recognized, and every number is verifiable from raw COMPRANET data published by the Mexican government.
error: Failed to load data. Please try again.
noData: No data available for this period.
